_meta.description: Per-terrain-type properties. 12 types across 5 layers; hardness increases with depth. Drill class effectiveness: lower value = class is stronger vs this terrain (faster dig). Bedrock is indestructible; Core Hollow Shell is the hardest DRILLABLE terrain (walls the Core Hollow, must be breached).
_meta.status: DEV PLACEHOLDERS — base_dig_time/move_speed_mod and ALL drill class_effectiveness multipliers are provisional TBD values chosen for testable feel. NOT final; pending the AI balance pass.
_meta.class_effectiveness_status: TBD DEV PLACEHOLDERS — every Precision/Burst/Thermal/Resonance multiplier is provisional. Direction: Precision→hard/dense, Burst→medium, Thermal→soft/organic, Resonance→balanced. Note: the dig calc currently SKIPS class_effectiveness for Thermal (DrillClassData.ignores_terrain_effectiveness), so Thermal's values are intent-only until that is revisited.
_meta.core_hollow_shell_status: TBD — base_dig_time 8.0 is a PLACEHOLDER, deliberately the highest of any drillable terrain (>2x Ultra Dense's 3.5) so breaching the Core Hollow takes noticeably longer than any other tile even for the best class/tier. Final resistance pending the AI balance pass; it must always remain the hardest drillable terrain (Bedrock stays indestructible and is not counted).

fields.base_dig_time: seconds for a Common drill of a neutral class to break one tile
fields.class_effectiveness: per drill-class multiplier on dig time (lower = the class is strong vs this terrain)
